title: Plan a Multi-Stop Trip
summary: Plan a route with multiple stops in the most efficient order.
learnerPromise: Plan a route with multiple stops in the most efficient order.
whyItMatters: Life is rarely a simple A-to-B trip. Errands, appointments, and social plans often mean multiple stops. Planning them well saves time.
realLifeUses: Run errands — bank, grocery store, post office — in one efficient loop.
Plan a day of college visits or apartment viewings across town.
Organize a road trip with sightseeing stops along the way.
Deliver items to multiple friends or family members in one outing.
youWillLearn: List all your stops and estimate time at each one.
Order your stops to minimize backtracking.
Factor in transit schedules, traffic, and opening hours.
Build in buffer time for delays.
miniChallenge: Plan a route to 2-3 places you need to go, ordering them by location.
steps: Write down every stop you need to make and what you will do there.
Estimate how long each stop takes and check opening hours.
Look at a map. Order your stops to create the shortest overall loop.
Check transit times or traffic conditions between each pair of stops.
Build in 10-15 minutes of buffer time, then follow the plan.
completionCriteria: You plan a route with 2-3 stops in an efficient order.
Your total travel time is less than doing the stops separately.
commonProblems: One stop takes longer than expected — build buffer time into your plan.
A location is closed — check hours before you leave.
You backtrack because the route order was wrong — map it before you move.
tips: Cluster nearby stops together to avoid criss-crossing the city.
Do the most time-sensitive stop first.
Keep a backup plan in case one stop gets delayed or canceled.
